tasks.subject: Task 0: Scaffold Next.js project with shadcn preset
tasks.description:
**Goal:** A running Next.js app at the repo root, initialized with the shadcn preset the user chose, committed to git.

**Files:**
- Create: entire Next.js scaffold at repo root (`app/`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `next.config.ts`, `components.json`, `app/globals.css`, …)
- Modify: `.gitignore` (merge generated ignores with existing `.superpowers/` entry)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `npm run dev` serves a page at http://localhost:3000 with no errors
- [ ] `components.json` exists (shadcn initialized) and `app/globals.css` contains the preset's theme variables
- [ ] `.gitignore` still ignores `.superpowers/`
- [ ] Scaffold committed to git

**Verify:** `npm run dev` → page renders at localhost:3000; `git log --oneline` shows scaffold commit.

**Steps:**
1. `npx shadcn@latest init --preset b1au69eka --template next --pointer -n portfolio` (choose npm/defaults at prompts)
2. Append `.superpowers/` to `portfolio/.gitignore`, `rm .gitignore`, `rsync -a portfolio/ ./`, `rm -rf portfolio`
3. `npm install && npm run dev` → confirm localhost:3000 renders, stop server
4. `npm run lint` → no errors
5. `git add -A && git commit -m "feat: scaffold Next.js app with shadcn preset"`

Full details: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-13-portfolio-site.md → Task 0

```json:metadata
{"files": [".gitignore", "package.json", "app/"], "verifyCommand": "npm run lint", "acceptanceCriteria": ["npm run dev serves localhost:3000 with no errors", "components.json exists and globals.css has preset theme variables", ".gitignore still ignores .superpowers/", "scaffold committed"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 1: Set up Vitest + React Testing Library
tasks.description:
**Goal:** A working test harness (`npm test`) with jsdom, RTL, and jest-dom matchers, proven by a passing smoke test.

**Files:**
- Create: `vitest.config.ts`, `vitest.setup.ts`, `__tests__/smoke.test.tsx`
- Modify: `package.json` (add `test` script)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `npm test` runs Vitest and the smoke test passes
- [ ] `@/` path alias resolves inside tests
- [ ] jest-dom matchers (e.g. `toBeInTheDocument`) available without per-file imports

**Verify:** `npm test` → 1 passed.

**Steps:**
1. `npm install -D vitest @vitejs/plugin-react jsdom @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom @testing-library/user-event`
2. Create `vitest.config.ts` (react plugin, jsdom env, globals, setupFiles ./vitest.setup.ts, `@` alias → repo root; mirror tsconfig paths if scaffold uses src/)
3. Create `vitest.setup.ts` with `import "@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest";`
4. Add `"test": "vitest run"` and `"test:watch": "vitest"` scripts
5. Write `__tests__/smoke.test.tsx` rendering a trivial component and asserting with jest-dom
6. `npm test` → PASS; commit

Full code in: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-13-portfolio-site.md → Task 1

```json:metadata
{"files": ["vitest.config.ts", "vitest.setup.ts", "__tests__/smoke.test.tsx", "package.json"], "verifyCommand": "npm test", "acceptanceCriteria": ["npm test runs and smoke test passes", "@/ alias resolves in tests", "jest-dom matchers available globally"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: Site config and project data
tasks.description:
**Goal:** All page content lives in two typed files — `lib/site.ts` (personal info) and `lib/projects.ts` (projects) — with a data-integrity test.

**Files:**
- Create: `lib/site.ts`, `lib/projects.ts`, `__tests__/projects.test.ts`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `Project` type has `name`, `description`, `status`, optional `videoUrl`, optional `href`
- [ ] Capture Studio and LC Finance are `shipped` with a video URL; WebSketch2 is `in-development` with no video
- [ ] Data-integrity test passes: every project has non-empty name/description ending in a period; every shipped project has a videoUrl
- [ ] Real values from the user (GitHub URL, LinkedIn URL, tagline, bio, project links) substituted before commit

**Verify:** `npm test` → all tests pass including `__tests__/projects.test.ts`.

**Steps:**
1. BLOCKING: coordinator asks the user for GitHub URL, LinkedIn URL, tagline, bio, and any live URLs/repos for the three projects. Do NOT invent these values.
2. TDD: write failing `__tests__/projects.test.ts` (≥3 projects; every project has name + punctuated description; every shipped project has videoUrl) → run, expect FAIL
3. Create `lib/site.ts` (name, tagline, email bradyshimanek33@gmail.com, github, linkedin, bio, url) with the user's real answers
4. Create `lib/projects.ts`: ProjectStatus/Project types; PLACEHOLDER_VIDEO = https://interactive-examples.mdn.mozilla.net/media/cc0-videos/flower.mp4; Capture Studio + LC Finance shipped w/ placeholder video; WebSketch2 in-development, no video; add href where user provided one
5. `npm test` → PASS; commit

Full code in: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-13-portfolio-site.md → Task 2

```json:metadata
{"files": ["lib/site.ts", "lib/projects.ts", "__tests__/projects.test.ts"], "verifyCommand": "npm test", "acceptanceCriteria": ["Project type with name/description/status/videoUrl?/href?", "Capture Studio and LC Finance shipped with video URL; WebSketch2 in-development without", "data-integrity test passes", "real user-provided content substituted, not invented"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: DemoVideo component
tasks.description:
**Goal:** A client component rendering a muted, looping video that lazy-loads, plays only while in the viewport, and falls back to manual controls under `prefers-reduced-motion`.

**Files:**
- Create: `components/demo-video.tsx`, `__tests__/demo-video.test.tsx`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Video renders with `muted`, `loop`, `playsInline`, `preload="none"`, and an aria-label naming the project (`"<title> demo"`)
- [ ] `play()` called when element intersects viewport; `pause()` when it leaves
- [ ] With `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: no IntersectionObserver autoplay; `controls` shown instead
- [ ] Observer disconnected on unmount

**Verify:** `npm test` → `__tests__/demo-video.test.tsx` passes (4 tests).

**Steps:**
1. TDD: write failing `__tests__/demo-video.test.tsx` — stub IntersectionObserver class capturing the callback, mock window.matchMedia, spy on HTMLMediaElement.prototype play/pause. Tests: attribute rendering; play on intersect + pause on leave; reduced-motion shows controls and skips observer; disconnect on unmount → run, expect FAIL
2. Implement `components/demo-video.tsx` ("use client"): matchMedia effect tracks prefers-reduced-motion; IntersectionObserver effect (threshold 0.25) plays/pauses videoRef, disconnects on cleanup, skipped when reducedMotion; `<video muted loop playsInline preload="none" controls={reducedMotion} aria-label={`${title} demo`} className="w-full rounded-md border border-border">`
3. `npm test` → PASS; commit

Full code in: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-13-portfolio-site.md → Task 3

```json:metadata
{"files": ["components/demo-video.tsx", "__tests__/demo-video.test.tsx"], "verifyCommand": "npm test", "acceptanceCriteria": ["video has muted/loop/playsInline/preload=none and aria-label", "plays on intersect, pauses on leave", "reduced motion: controls shown, no autoplay observer", "observer disconnected on unmount"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: ProjectEntry and ProjectList components
tasks.description:
**Goal:** The Projects section — a quiet list where each entry shows name, description, optional external link, an "In development" badge when applicable, and an inline demo video when one exists.

**Files:**
- Create: `components/project-entry.tsx`, `components/project-list.tsx`, `__tests__/project-entry.test.tsx`
- Create (via CLI): `components/ui/badge.tsx`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Entry with `href` renders name as a link; without `href`, plain text
- [ ] "In development" badge appears only for `status: "in-development"`
- [ ] Video renders only when `videoUrl` is present
- [ ] ProjectList renders one entry per project from `lib/projects.ts` under a "Projects" heading

**Verify:** `npm test` → `__tests__/project-entry.test.tsx` passes (5 tests).

**Steps:**
1. `npx shadcn@latest add badge`
2. TDD: write failing `__tests__/project-entry.test.tsx` with shipped + in-dev fixtures — link vs plain-text name; badge only for in-dev; video only with videoUrl; ProjectList renders all projects under Projects heading. Reuse the IntersectionObserver/matchMedia stubs from demo-video tests in beforeEach → run, expect FAIL
3. Implement `components/project-entry.tsx`: name (link if href), `— description` in muted text, outline Badge "In development" when in-dev, DemoVideo below when videoUrl
4. Implement `components/project-list.tsx`: section aria-labelledby, uppercase tracking-widest "Projects" h2, ul of entries with border-t dividers (first: no divider)
5. `npm test` → PASS; commit

Full code in: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-13-portfolio-site.md → Task 4

```json:metadata
{"files": ["components/project-entry.tsx", "components/project-list.tsx", "components/ui/badge.tsx", "__tests__/project-entry.test.tsx"], "verifyCommand": "npm test", "acceptanceCriteria": ["href renders name as link, otherwise plain text", "badge only for in-development", "video only when videoUrl present", "ProjectList renders every project under Projects heading"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: Page assembly — header, about, layout, dark typographic styling
tasks.description:
**Goal:** The finished single page: dark-only, Geist Mono type, Header → Projects → About, with complete metadata/OG tags.

**Files:**
- Create: `components/site-header.tsx`, `components/about.tsx`
- Modify: `app/layout.tsx`, `app/page.tsx` (and `app/globals.css` if font mapping needed)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Page renders Header (name, tagline, GitHub · LinkedIn · Email), Projects, About in order
- [ ] Dark theme forced (`<html class="dark">`); no light mode flash
- [ ] Monospace font applied site-wide
- [ ] `<title>`, description, and Open Graph tags present in built HTML
- [ ] Layout centered, max-width column, responsive at 375px width

**Verify:** `npm test && npm run build` succeed; visual check at localhost:3000.

**Steps:**
1. `components/site-header.tsx`: h1 name, muted tagline, nav with GitHub · LinkedIn · Email (mailto) underlined links from lib/site
2. `components/about.tsx`: uppercase tracking-widest "About" h2 + muted bio paragraph
3. Rewrite `app/page.tsx`: `<main className="mx-auto max-w-2xl px-6 py-16 sm:py-24">` with SiteHeader, ProjectList, About
4. Rewrite `app/layout.tsx`: Geist_Mono via next/font/google; metadata from lib/site (title, description, metadataBase, openGraph); `<html lang="en" className="dark">`; body `font-mono bg-background text-foreground antialiased`. If preset doesn't map font-mono to the variable, add `@theme inline { --font-mono: var(--font-geist-mono); }` (Tailwind v4) or extend tailwind.config.ts (v3)
5. `npm test && npm run build` → all pass
6. Visual check: dark bg, mono type, section order, videos autoplay in view, WebSketch2 badge, 375px responsive, title/og tags in page source
7. Commit

Full code in: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-13-portfolio-site.md → Task 5

```json:metadata
{"files": ["components/site-header.tsx", "components/about.tsx", "app/layout.tsx", "app/page.tsx", "app/globals.css"], "verifyCommand": "npm test && npm run build", "acceptanceCriteria": ["header/projects/about render in order", "dark theme forced via html.dark", "monospace site-wide", "title/description/OG tags in built HTML", "centered max-width column responsive at 375px"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 6: Production readiness — README, Supabase instructions, deploy notes
tasks.description:
**Goal:** A clean production build plus a README documenting how to swap placeholder videos for Supabase-hosted demos and how to deploy to Vercel.

**Files:**
- Create: `README.md`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] README covers: local dev commands, Supabase Storage setup (public `demos` bucket, upload, public URL format, where to paste URLs in lib/projects.ts), video export specs (MP4 H.264, 1080p, 60fps, no audio, 15–30s loop, <10MB), Vercel deploy steps
- [ ] `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` all pass from a clean state

**Verify:** `npm run lint && npm test && npm run build` → all succeed.

**Steps:**
1. Write README.md per the plan's full text (dev commands; content-as-code note; Supabase bucket walkthrough with URL format `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/demos/<file>.mp4`; video export specs; Vercel import + optional custom domain)
2. `npm run lint && npm test && npm run build` → all succeed
3. Commit

Full text in: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-13-portfolio-site.md → Task 6

```json:metadata
{"files": ["README.md"], "verifyCommand": "npm run lint && npm test && npm run build", "acceptanceCriteria": ["README covers dev commands, Supabase Storage setup, video export specs, Vercel deploy", "lint + test + build all pass clean"]}
```
